inline task_statement body at materialization time","task_source_ref":"task-source:inline-statement","task_statement":"# F10: review gates RECORD the grounding fact ref without DEMANDING it (demand/record split)

Observed: notify-customer-language-autowire-0612 closed with review QA returns whose evidence_used carried NO repository artifacts, yet its sufficiency traces RECORD 'BrickComparisonFact...evidence_used.repository_artifact_ref' — the gate forwarded (no demand) while the lifecycle resolver cannot resolve the recorded ref. Two dated registry entries (PRE_GROUNDING_LAW_BUILDING_IDS in check_building_lifecycle_path_shape.py) currently grandfather this transition; YOUR repair closes the class so no third entry is ever needed.

REQUIRED: unify demand and record through ONE predicate/source (house principle): a gate that RECORDS the grounding fact ref must have DEMANDED it (missing artifacts -> missing required facts -> the declared gate behavior fires: hold/reroute per policy), and a gate that does not demand for a given return shape must NOT record the ref. Inspect support/operator/run.py (~1545-1574), gate_sequence.py (~80-83, 250-255), plan_validation.py grounding fact declaration (~78-87, 596-656) and make the demand-set and record-set provably identical (single shared function both sides call).
PROOF: temp drives: (a) review return WITHOUT artifacts -> gate reports missing required fact (does NOT forward silently) and no unresolvable ref is recorded; (b) review return WITH artifacts -> forwards and the recorded ref resolves; (c) non-review shape -> no grounding demand, no grounding ref recorded. FIRE: mutate the shared predicate out of one side -> checker RED (add/extend pin).
Full gate -> exit 0 (say which checkout/copy). Constraints: support/* only; fails-closed; no pin weakening; do not touch the two dated registry entries (history).
"}
